$comment: Hand-authored taxonomy. Powers the Topic Front Door (/t, /t/:topic) and the section list inside each state hub (/s/:state). See ADR-0022 and TODO/IA-RESET-PLACE-FIRST-WITH-TOPIC-FRONT-DOOR.md. DOCTRINE (user-mandated 2026-05-11): elections are ONE indicator family among many. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are not the spine and not the default theme. Order in this array IS the rendering order on /t and inside the state hub.

topics.title: Fiscal capacity
topics.summary: How states raise, borrow, and spend. Debt, deficit, transfers from the Centre. The baseline that contextualises every other state-level number.
topics.notes: Debt and deficit are co-determined by central transfers — the transfers indicator is the missing context that makes ranked tables honest. Tracked under the IA reset's ingest gate (TODO/IA-RESET-PLACE-FIRST-WITH-TOPIC-FRONT-DOOR.md §Phased migration).

topics.title: Energy
topics.summary: Where electricity is generated. Installed capacity by state, by fuel.
topics.notes: Installed-MW is a *siting* statistic, not a consumption statistic — the state where a plant sits is not necessarily the state that consumes the power. The indicator artifact's `attribution_geography` field carries this caveat to the renderer.

topics.title: Elections
topics.summary: Who governs. State Assembly and Lok Sabha results, candidate-level detail, party totals, margins. One indicator family among many — not the spine of the site.
topics.notes: Per ADR-0022 doctrine: elections are NOT the default home-page theme and NOT a featured topic. Social-welfare topics (fiscal, future: education, health, livelihood) are first-class; elections are one of many. Election artifacts have a different schema family (result.summary.schema.json) than indicators — the catalogue's polymorphic `kind` lets them coexist without being demoted in capability or promoted in IA prominence.
